item.name: Delete
GitHub Delete Repository Webhook

request.name: GitHub Delete Repository Webhook

description.content: This API operation deletes a specific webhook from a GitHub repository by sending a DELETE request to the endpoint /repos/{owner}/{repo}/hooks/{hook_id}. The operation requires three path parameters: the repository owner's username, the repository name, and the unique identifier of the webhook to be removed. Upon successful execution, the webhook is permanently deleted from the repository, meaning it will no longer receive or trigger events for that repository. This operation typically requires appropriate authentication and repository admin permissions to execute, and it returns a 204 No Content status code when the deletion is successful.

item.name: GitHub Get Pre-receive Hook for Repository

request.name: GitHub Get Pre-receive Hook for Repository

description.content: This API endpoint retrieves detailed information about a specific pre-receive hook configured for a GitHub repository. Pre-receive hooks are scripts that run on the GitHub server before code is accepted into a repository, allowing administrators to enforce quality standards or policies. By providing the repository owner, repository name, and the pre-receive hook ID in the URL path, this GET operation returns comprehensive details about that particular hook, including its configuration, enforcement level, and current status within the specified repository.

variable.description: (Required) The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

(Required) The unique identifier of the pre-receive hook.

description.content: This API operation deletes a specific webhook from a GitHub repository by sending a DELETE request to the endpoint /repos/{owner}/{repo}/hooks/{hook_id}. The operation requires three path parameters: the repository owner's username, the repository name, and the unique identifier of the webhook to be removed. Upon successful execution, the webhook is permanently deleted from the repository, meaning it will no longer receive or trigger events for that repository. This operation typically requires appropriate authentication and repository admin permissions to execute, and it returns a 204 No Content status code when the deletion is successful.

description.content: This API operation deletes a specific webhook from a GitHub repository by sending a DELETE request to the endpoint /repos/{owner}/{repo}/hooks/{hook_id}. The operation requires three path parameters: the repository owner's username, the repository name, and the unique identifier of the webhook to be removed. Upon successful execution, the webhook is permanently deleted from the repository, meaning it will no longer receive or trigger events for that repository. This operation typically requires appropriate authentication and repository admin permissions to execute, and it returns a 204 No Content status code when the deletion is successful.

description.content: This will trigger a [ping event](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/webhooks/#ping-event) to be sent to the hook.

variable.description: (Required) The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
(Required) The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.
(Required) The unique identifier of the hook. You can find this value in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

item.name: GitHub Test the Push Repository Webhook

request.name: GitHub Test the Push Repository Webhook

description.content:
This will trigger the hook with the latest push to the current repository if the hook is subscribed to `push` events. If the hook is not subscribed to `push` events, the server will respond with 204 but no test POST will be generated.

**Note**: Previously `/repos/:owner/:repo/hooks/:hook_id/test`
